author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! āra ki emana daśā haba
chāḍiyā puruṣadeha, kabe vā prakṛti haba,
du̐hu aṅge candana parāba

ṭāniyā bā̐dhiba cūḍā, nava guñjāhāre beḍā,
nānā phule gā̐thi diba hāra
pītavasana aṅge, parāiba sakhī⋅saṅge,
vadane tāmbūla diba āra

du̐hu rūpa manohārī, heriba nayana bhari’,
nīlāmbare rāi sājāiyā
navaratna⋅jari āni, bāndhiba vicitra veṇī,
tāte phula mālatī gā̐thiyā

sei rūpamādhurī, dekhiba nayana bhari’,
ei kari mane abhilāṣa
jaya rūpa sanātana, deha more ei dhana,
nivedaye narottama⋅dāsa
en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Will such a state ever be mine? Giving up this male body and becoming a female attendant, I will adorn the bodies of both Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa with sandalwood paste.

I will tightly bind Their hair into a topknot, encircling it with a fresh garland of berries. I will weave garlands of various flowers. Along with the companions, I will dress Them in yellow garments and offer betel to Their mouths.

I will gaze to my heart's content upon Their enchanting forms, dressing Rādhā in blue garments. Bringing lace of nine jewels, I will braid Her hair into an exquisite braid, weaving jasmine flowers into it.

I will behold that beautiful sweetness to my heart's content; this is the ardent desire within my heart. All glories to Rūpa and Sanātana! Bestow this treasure upon me—thus prays Narottama dāsa.
